speakers.name: Vikas Jadhav
speakers.summary: Vikas Jadhav opened the earnings call by welcoming participants and introducing the key speakers, Venu Lambu and Vipul Chandra. He set the stage for the call by mentioning the possibility of forward-looking statements and the inherent risks and uncertainties associated with them. His tone was neutral, focusing on procedural aspects of the call rather than specific financial details or projections.

speakers.name: Venu Lambu
speakers.role: Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director
direct_answers: Yes, thanks for the question, Sandeep. Look, I think if you look at it across, all our client buckets, we have actually grown.
dodged_or_vague: FY2027 is still some time away to comment, so let us hold on to that.
speakers.summary: Venu Lambu expressed confidence in LTIMindtree's strong performance in Q2 FY2026, highlighting the company's commitment to becoming an AI-centric organization. He emphasized the success of their strategy, which includes sales transformation and a focus on large deals. Venu was optimistic about sustaining profitable growth through strategic transformation and AI investments. He directly addressed questions about client growth but was vague about future projections for FY2027, indicating a cautious approach to long-term forecasting.

speakers.name: Vipul Chandra
direct_answers: Sure. So I think the answer to that question is that until last quarter we were accounting for this entity that we had set up in partnership with Aramco as a joint venture and equity method consolidation.
dodged_or_vague: We do not give out our specific EBIT guidance for medium term, so I will refrain from doing that.
speakers.summary: Vipul Chandra provided a detailed overview of LTIMindtree's financial performance for Q2 FY2026, highlighting revenue growth and an expansion in EBIT margins. He attributed the margin improvement to the Fit for Future initiative and favorable forex conditions. Vipul confidently addressed questions about financial specifics, such as the consolidation of a joint venture, but avoided giving specific medium-term EBIT guidance, indicating a cautious approach to future financial projections.

speakers.name: Moderator
speakers.summary: The moderator facilitated the earnings call, ensuring a smooth transition between speakers and managing the Q&A session. The tone was neutral, focusing on procedural aspects and ensuring participants were aware of the call's format and recording.

speakers.name: Sandeep Shah
direct_answers: Yes, thanks for the question, Sandeep. Look, I think if you look at it across, all our client buckets, we have actually grown.
speakers.summary: Sandeep Shah asked probing questions about the growth trajectory of LTIMindtree's top clients and potential long-term concerns. His inquiries were direct, seeking clarity on client-specific growth and its impact on the company's overall growth journey. Sandeep's tone was inquisitive, aiming to understand the nuances of client relationships and their implications for future performance.

chain_of_thought: The analysis begins by identifying each speaker and their role in the earnings call. Key statements are extracted for each speaker, focusing on verbatim quotes that highlight specific language, numbers, and forward-looking statements. The tone of each speaker is assessed based on their statements and interactions during the call. For management speakers, the analysis notes which analyst questions were answered directly and which were deflected or avoided. A narrative summary is crafted for each speaker, capturing their contribution to the call, stance, and key messages. The JSON structure is used to organize this information systematically, ensuring clarity and coherence in the analysis.
